title: OLED-Info Pro launches its first Video Podcast: industry updates, and expert insights
content: We’re happy to share that our second OLED-Info Pro video lecture is now live — and it’s something new: our first-ever OLED-Info Pro Video Podcast!

title: Reports suggest Apple reduces its foldable OLED orders from Samsung, signs an exclusive agreement
content: Apple is moving forward with its first foldable OLED iPhone project, that will launch later in 2026, and it was previously reported that the US company has ordered 13 million units from SDC towards the end of 2025. Apple originally ordered 11 million units, but has recently added 2 million more as it anticipates high demand for its new device.

title: Remember LG's rollable smartphone that almost launched in 2021? You can now watch a teardown of one of the prototypes produced back then
content:
Some years ago LG Electronics aimed to be the first company to release a rollable smartphone, hoping to launch it by the end of 2021. Later in 2021 LG Electronics closed its smartphone business, and the rollable OLED phone project was scrapped.

title: Samsung Display announced it has shipped over 5 million QD-OLED monitor panels
content:
Samsung Display announced today that it has shipped over 5 million QD-OLED monitor panels last month, about 4 years after the company commenced the mass production of QD-OLED monitor panels.

title: Counterpoint: OLED laptop shiupments to grow 33% in 2026 and gain market share over miniLEDs
content: Counterpoint says that laptop display shipments grew 7% in 2025, and the advanced (OLED and miniLED) laptop segment grew 19%, driven by expanded adoption of AI PCs, upgrades at enterprises - and demand pull-forward ahead of memory cost inflation.

title: Samsung Display reportedly achieve 80% yields at its A6 8.6-Gen production line, on track for mass production in June
content: A few weeks ago we reported that Samsung Display may be delaying the A6 8.6-Gen ramp up, following some technical challenges - and demand concerns. Today it is reported that say that this it not the case - SDC actually managed to achieve yields of over 80%, and is on track to start full mass production by June 2026.
